data.title: Ntracts promotes execs for healthcare growth, strengthens team across services, sales, product, operations, innovation & strategy.
data.description: Ntracts, based in Chattanooga, Tennessee, has recently made significant executive leadership promotions to bolster its support for healthcare organizations in adapting to rising complexities. The company has elevated key individuals within its executive team in various departments including client services, sales, product development, operations, innovation, and strategy. These promotions signify Ntracts' dedication to strategic growth, operational excellence, and the creation of impactful solutions for healthcare professionals. The company is focused on delivering genuine value to healthcare teams through these changes.

data.title: Tumble 22's new Pflugerville location opens Jan 31, 2026, known for hot chicken, cold beer, and cheap drinks.
data.description: In Pflugerville, Texas, a new location of the beloved Texas Chicken Joint, Tumble 22, is set to open its doors on January 31, 2026. This Austin-born eatery is famous for its combination of cold beer, hot chicken, and inexpensive, strong drinks. The team at Tumble 22 is eagerly anticipating the opportunity to serve the local community and provide their signature offerings.

data.title: Bank of America declares dividends for preferred stock series, payable in Feb and March 2026 - PRNewswire.
data.description: In Charlotte, North Carolina, on January 16, 2026, Bank of America Corporation revealed that its Board of Directors has approved the payment of regular cash dividends for various series of preferred stock. The dividends will be distributed per share or depositary share for the specified series of preferred stock. The precise dividend amounts were not provided in the announcement.

data.title: Turbo Energy (Nasdaq: TURB) faces Nasdaq notice for non-compliance with stockholders' equity requirement, received on Jan. 12, 2026.
data.description: In Valencia, Spain, on January 16, 2026, Turbo Energy S.A. (Nasdaq: TURB), a leading provider of AI-optimized solar energy storage technologies, disclosed receiving a written notice from The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC on January 12, 2026. The notice highlighted that the company is not in compliance with Nasdaq Capital Market continued listing requirements. This development could potentially impact the operations of Turbo Energy, which operates globally.

data.title: Zenas BioPharma granted employee 100,000 stock options on Jan 15, 2026 as an inducement to join the company.
data.description: On January 15, 2026, Zenas BioPharma, Inc. in Waltham, Mass., revealed that the Compensation Committee of the Company’s Board of Directors issued 100,000 non-qualified stock options to a new employee as part of an inducement for joining the company. Zenas, traded on Nasdaq as ZBIO, focuses on developing and commercializing autoimmune disease therapies. The stock options were granted in compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4) to encourage the new employee's employment with the company.

data.title: Silgan will report Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings on Feb 4, 2026; Await financial results.
data.description: Silgan Corporation, based in Norwalk, Connecticut, has announced that they will be revealing their financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025 on February 4, 2026. This disclosure will include crucial figures regarding their earnings performance during that period.

data.title: Dynex Capital, Inc. (NYSE: DX) to release Q4 and full year 2025 financial results before market open on Jan 26, 2026. Conference call at 10:00 a.m. ET. Website for webcast: www.dynexcapital.com.
data.description: GLEN ALLEN, VA. - Dynex Capital, Inc. disclosed that it is set to unveil its financial outcomes for the fourth quarter and entire year 2025 prior to the market opening. Subsequently, the company will conduct a conference call and live audio webcast to review the financial results at 10:00 a.m. ET on Monday, January 26, 2026. Interested parties can tune into the live audio webcast through www.dynexcapital.com on the Investors page. An archive of the webcast will also be posted on the Company's website after the event. Dynex Capital, Inc. operates under the NYSE as DX.

data.title: First Western Trust invests in arts with digital installation in Boulder, showing dedication to community well-being.
data.description: Denver-based First Western Trust has prioritized community development since its establishment in 2004. The firm's dedication to philanthropy, economic growth, and cultural advancement is evident in its consistent support for the arts sector. Serving as a foundation of the bank's ethos, this commitment aligns with its goal of transcending traditional financial services to become a collaborative force in enhancing community prosperity.

data.title: Coherent unveils germanium-free EOM for high-speed CO2 laser, ideal for via drilling.
data.description: Coherent has introduced a cutting-edge electro-optic modulator (EOM) tailored for rapid switching of CO₂ lasers in via drilling scenarios. This new technology boasts advanced features that enhance efficiency and precision in high-speed applications. With this innovation, Coherent aims to revolutionize the performance of CO₂ lasers in the industry, promising to deliver unparalleled speed and accuracy for via drilling tasks.

data.title: Cardlytics grants 1,000,000 restricted stock units to CFO David Evans as inducement under Nasdaq Listing Rule.
data.description: ATLANTA--Cardlytics, Inc. shared that on January 12, 2026, the Compensation Committee of Cardlytics’ Board of Directors awarded a total of 1,000,000 restricted stock units to David Evans, the newly appointed Chief Financial Officer. These stock units were given as a significant part of Evans' employment agreement with Cardlytics, following Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4), and were issued under the Cardlytics, Inc. 2022 Inducement Plan.

data.title: Small-cap stocks outperformed with Russell 2000 hitting record highs. U.S. economy expected to grow at 5.3% annual rate. Initial jobless claims low at 198,000.
data.description: Last week on Wall Street, the large-cap indexes remained steady while small-cap stocks saw a broadened rally, leading the Russell 2000 index to reach new record highs. This surge marked its longest streak of consecutive outperformance against the S&P 500 since 2008. The small-cap strength was supported by positive macroeconomic data, forecasting a remarkable 5.3% annual growth rate in the U.S. economy for the final quarter of 2025 as per the Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model. Furthermore, initial jobless claims totaled 198,000, falling below projections and marking the second-lowest level in two years, allaying concerns of a slowdown in the labor market. Analysts anticipate this unexpected economic acceleration to boost earnings for small and mid-cap companies, consequently prompting an upward adjustment in profit forecasts. For more details, refer to the latest article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Metallus and USW Local 1123 reach tentative 4-year contract agreement in Canton, Ohio. Characters: 91
data.description: In Canton, Ohio, on January 16, 2026, Metallus, a company listed on NYSE as MTUS and known for its specialty metals, components, and supply chain solutions, has announced a new four-year tentative agreement with the United Steelworkers (USW) Local 1123. This agreement comes after negotiations with the members of USW Local 1123.

data.title: A2Z Cust2Mate Solutions Corp. (NASDAQ: AZ) has received a deficiency notification from Nasdaq regarding its annual meeting.
data.description: On January 13, 2026, A2Z Cust2Mate Solutions Corp. (NASDAQ: AZ), a prominent player in smart retail technology, disclosed receiving a notification from The Nasdaq regarding its listing qualifications.
